channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); currently independent (previously Shopify, then Reddit); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: All about Information Retrieval & Search
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: LambdaMART
item.description: LambdaMART Stub. Created as a placeholder — expand with vault-sourced content.

item.title: Learning to Rank
item.description: Learning to Rank Definition Learning to Rank (LTR) is the application of machine learning to the ranking problem in information retrieval — training models to produce optimal rankings given a query and a set of candidate documents, using labeled (query, document, relevance) training data.

item.title: NDCG (Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain)
item.description: NDCG (Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain) Definition NDCG is the most widely used ranking quality metric in information retrieval.

item.title: Position Bias
item.description: Position Bias Definition Position bias is the tendency for users to click on higher-ranked search results independent of their actual relevance — simply because they appear earlier.

item.title: History
item.description: Awesome Search — Notes History Chronological log of notes added to this knowledge graph. Newest first.

item.title: Index
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: Roman Grebennikov
item.description: Roman Grebennikov Engineer and co-creator/maintainer of Metarank, the open-source LTR secondary re-ranker.

item.title: Personalization in Search
item.description: Personalization in Search Personalization adapts search results to the individual user — their history, preferences, context, and intent — rather than returning the same ranked list for everyone.
